sglang.title: remove redundant pad_input_ids function
sglang.author: amosyou
sglang.bodyExcerpt: pad_input_ids in LlavaQwenForCausalLM can be inherited from LlavaLlamaForCausalLM

sglang.title: Litellm Backend
sglang.author: huyiwen
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Example
import sglang as sgl
from sglang import LiteLLM, function, set_default_backend


@function
def multi_turn_question(s, question):
    s += sgl.system("You are a helpful assistant.")
    s += sgl.user(question)
    s += sgl.assistant_begin()
    s += " Let's think step by step. "
    s += sgl.

sglang.title: Fix rid state map leak + Refractor .finished
sglang.author: Qubitium
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
HIGH IMPACT: @ZhouXingg Fixed a bug/memory leak of rid state map when stream=False.  Rid state map cleanup code after yield is not executed since the receiver of the generator early returns and never calls it again. Qubitium@3407263
MEDIUM IMPACT: The rest of the changes deal with merging all struct

sglang.title: Crash the server when error or OOM happens
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Otherwise, the requests will hang forever.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Switch fp8 layers to use the CUTLASS kernels
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Switching from torch._scaled_mm to vLLM's cutlass fp8 kernels when supported as we are seeing 5-15% improvement in e2e performance on neuralmagic/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct-FP8
see https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1GiAnmzyGHgZ6zL_LDSTm35Bdrt4A8AaFEurDlISYYA4/ for some quick e2e benchmarks and #5

vllm.title: Remove Ray health check
vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The Ray health check code uses a private API that is to be deprecated. Furthermore, it introduces unacceptable overhead in async context. This PR removes this health check. Note that should a Ray Worker die, the exception will be raised in next engine step regardless, which has nothing to do with th

vllm.title: Addition of lacked ignored_seq_groups in _schedule_chunked_prefill
vllm.author: JamesLim-sy
vllm.bodyExcerpt: I think infeasible_seq_groups generated by _schedule_swapped should be added into ignored_seq_groups of SchedulerOutputs

vllm.title: [Kernel] Dynamic Per-Token Activation Quantization
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Adds an additional CompressedTensorsScheme to support w8a8 models with dynamic per-token activation quantization, CompressedTensorsW8A8DynamicToken. This scheme adds support for w8a8 dynamic per-token models quantized through sparseml and saved through compressed-tensors
Expands CompressedT

vllm.title: [Frontend] Add OpenAI Vision API Support
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is the final step of the release milestone of adding support for GPT4V compatible inference.
This PR fixes

#2058
#3873
#4205
#4826

and closes #4200

vllm.title: [Misc] Remove unused cuda_utils.h in CPU backend
vllm.author: DamonFool
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hi all,
May I get reviews for this trivial change?
cuda_utils.h was included in the CPU backend, which seems to be unreasonable.
Fortunately, it can be removed without regression, which is actually unused.
Thanks.
Best regards,
Jie

vllm.title: [Bug Fix] Fix the support check for FP8 CUTLASS
vllm.author: cli99
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Bug description:
With torch 2.4.0.dev20240603+cu121,
cutlass_fp8_supported outputs False, and the (capability, version) before the comparison is (90, 11111111112)
This PR fixes the support check for FP8 CUTLASS ( cutlass_fp8_supported) which was introduced in #5183.

vllm.title: [Misc] Add args for selecting distributed executor to benchmarks
vllm.author: BKitor
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Add arguments for --distributed-executor-backend to the benchmark_throughput.py / benchmark_latency.py scripts.

vllm.title: [ROCm][AMD] Use pytorch sdpa math backend to do naive attention
vllm.author: hongxiayang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This pull request uses pytorch sdpa math backend to replace the existing naive attention in ROCm, as it shows latency improvement (e.g. prefill latency) over the current naive attention based on latency benchmarking.
The naive attention is desirable (like in GPUs where support for ck flash-attention
